title: centaurea margaritacea subsp. proto-margaritacea
intro: Centaurea margaritacea subsp. proto-margaritacea, also known as proto-margaritaceous cornflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Centaurea margaritacea subsp. proto-margaritacea include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the flowers and leaves, which can then be infused into teas or made into capsules for medicinal use.
